usage: check_noise [-help] [-all] [-check_only {receiver_no_propagation_model receiver_no_dc_tolerence driver_no_current_source_model driver_no_model}] [-instance_list <string>] [-instance_only {true false}] [-pin_list <string>] [-type {receiver_model driver_model}] [-unique {true false}] [-verbose] [-view <string>] [ [ > | >> ] ]

options.name: -check_only
options.description: {receiver_no_propagation_model receiver_no_dc_tolerence driver_no_current_source_model driver_no_model} Checks the warning name. Following warnings are checked: receiver_no_propagation_model : Specifies how many design pins are missing the receiver model for propagation. receiver_no_dc_tolerence : Specifies how many pins have no DC VH/VL tolerance specified. driver_no_current_source_model : Specifies how many design pins are missing from the current source model. driver_no_model : Specifies how many design pins are missing from the driver model.

options.name: -instance_list
options.description: Specifies a list of instance names

options.name: -type
options.description: Checks the presence of a valid model type - receiver_model or driver_model. If the specified model is missing, a warning will be issued.

options.name: -unique
options.description: Report unique cells or pins for each power domain

options.name: -view
options.description: Specifies the view name (string, optional) > <filename> # Redirect output to file (redirect_operator, optional) >> <filename> # Append to output file
